summary: A superb sound‑board capture of the legendary May 15 1970 double‑show – crisp overall with a few early‑set mix quirks, featuring marathon performances and standout jams on The Other One, Dark Star and Attics.
review: The recording is a well‑preserved sound‑board tape that sounds clear and balanced for most of the show; only the very first acoustic set suffers from occasional channel loss and a slightly loud vocal mix. Musically the concert is an epic marathon – two full Dead sets, two New Riders segments and rare Pigpen organ moments – with The Other One, Dark Star, Attics, Candyman and Cold Jordan repeatedly singled out as highlights. Reviewers are uniformly enthusiastic, noting both the historic setlist flow and the high‑energy playing of Jerry, Bob and Pigpen.

ai_rating.rationale: Over 30 reviews overwhelmingly rate the show 4–5 stars; the performance is described as legendary with many standout songs, while the recording is praised as crisp sound‑board despite minor early‑set glitches.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Crisp mix and minimal hiss for most of the set; early acoustic portion has a missing right channel, occasional fade‑ins/out and vocals that are a bit high. No major dropouts reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A historic double‑bill with two acoustic sets, two New Riders interludes and two electric Dead halves; the pacing builds from gentle acoustic numbers into soaring second‑set jams, creating a marathon that many reviewers call "epic" and "quintessential Dead".

band_member_comments.Jerry: Repeatedly praised for his guitar tone and lead work (e.g., “Jer basically says game on” on The Other One).
band_member_comments.Phil: A few reviewers note that Phil’s bass is hard to hear in parts of the mix, but no specific performance criticism.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir gets kudos for vocal harmonies, his guest spot on Mama Tried/Sawmill and a “sweet Uncle John” jam during Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Keys: Pigpen’s organ and vocals are highlighted as clear and powerful; occasional comments about the early acoustic piano mix but overall positive.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann (referred to as Billiam) and Mickey Hart receive praise for solid, driving rhythms; no major drumming issues mentioned.
